LT78-2CS: Willem Lubberts van Westervelt was born in Meppel, in the Dutch province of Drenthe, in 1610. He crossed the Atlantic and arrived in New York City on 29 June 1662, joining the Dutch community taking root in the colony. He lived out his remaining years in the New World, dying in 1697 at Flatlands, Brooklyn.
9FMQ-D4P: John William Landrum was born in Aberdeen, Scotland, in 1635, and eventually made his home in the colony of Virginia. He married first in 1660, and was wed twice more, in 1684 and 1689, both times in Essex, Virginia. Records place him back in England in 1696, and he died in 1707 in St. Anne's Parish, Essex, Virginia.
MYM6-RG5: Peter Ware Sr was born in Dublin, Ireland, in 1605, and arrived in Virginia in 1657. He worked as an attorney in York, Virginia, a profession recorded in 1675. He died in 1659 at Hampton, in the Elizabeth City area of colonial Virginia.
L8YQ-PW7: Martha Costen was born in 1623 in Godalming, Surrey, England, and lived her whole life in that same town, where she died in 1677.
LCQ8-1C2: Cecelia Sussele was born in Quebec City in 1694 and made her way south into the British colonies. She lived for a time at Hempstead, in Queens, New York, and died in 1739 in the town of Poughkeepsie, in Dutchess County, New York.
KT72-RQM: Edward McGlamery was born in 1650 in Kent, Maryland, in British Colonial America. He drew up his will on 25 March 1721 in Somerset, Maryland, where he died two years later in 1723.
L71V-GGL: John Campbell was born in 1706 in Kirkoswald, Ayrshire, Scotland. He later emigrated to the American colonies, and died in 1790 at Bristol, in Bucks County, Pennsylvania.
LHYL-84B: John J. Smithson was born in Northumberland, England, in 1680, and lived for a time at Skelton in Cumberland. A Quaker by faith, he eventually crossed the Atlantic and settled in Virginia, where he died in 1761 in Lunenburg County.
KZYK-KC6: Johann Abraham Grimm was born in 1717 in Kusel, Bavaria, Germany, and was of the Reformed faith. He lived his entire life in his hometown of Kusel, where he died in 1787.
LYFP-VYS: Anna Catharina Heinrich was born in 1654 in Kusel, in the Rhineland-Palatinate region of Germany. She lived in the Palatinate, and died in her hometown of Kusel in 1724.
MB99-SGH: Anna Margaretha Heyden was baptized on 8 May 1626 in Hall, Württemberg, Germany, and lived there throughout her life. She was buried in Hall on 15 September 1652. Surviving records about her dates and family have proven difficult to reconcile, leaving some uncertainty about how her life connects to her parents and children.
LZVN-BVM: Christiana Baker was born in London, England, in 1634, and later crossed the Atlantic to the Massachusetts Bay Colony. She died in 1685 in Boston.
9N55-PSZ: Willemje Mattyse Thyssen was born in 1642 at Fort Orange in New Netherland. On 23 September 1663 she and Roelof Willemszen "van Heerden" were recorded among the members of the Old First Reformed Dutch Church in Brooklyn. She died in 1689 in Bergen, in colonial New Jersey.
PQFR-R34: Martha Corwin was born in 1632 in Ipswich, Massachusetts, and later lived in Southold on Long Island, New York, where she died in 1698.
MKW5-F1H: Thomas Gaines was born in 1584 in Breconshire, Wales. In 1634 he immigrated to America aboard the ships 'The Ark and The Dove', settling in colonial Virginia, where he died in 1640.
L17H-VJY: John Larkin was born in 1615 in Cardington, Bedfordshire, England, and became one of the early Quakers of the Anne Arundel area of Maryland. As early as 1658 he was punished for refusing to swear an oath of allegiance, and like many of his neighbors he had likely first come to Virginia as a Puritan before seeking the more tolerant climate of Maryland. He made his living as an innkeeper, planter, and land speculator, and tended to settle disputes by quietly paying debts rather than fighting in court. He died in 1702 in Anne Arundel, Maryland.
LZNL-WWV: Niklaus Jost was born in 1615 in the Bern region of Switzerland and baptized that 5 November. He died in 1658 in Wynigen, Bern.
KNZ4-XLS: Patrick Cain Sr. was born in 1712 in Ballaugh on the Isle of Man. He later crossed the Atlantic to the American South, and died in 1799 in Barnwell, South Carolina.
LC5G-R71: Simon Roberts was born in 1630 in Boston, in the Massachusetts Bay Colony, and died in 1680 in Gloucester, Essex County, Massachusetts.
L5TH-GKP: Earl Canute was born in 1696 in Southampton, Hampshire, England, and belonged to the Church of England. He raised sheep in Northumberland before emigrating to America, and died in 1772 at Elk Creek in Grayson County, Virginia.
49RC-XXR: Elizabeth Skelderman was born in 1678 in Richmond County, Virginia, and lived her whole life there, dying in the same county in 1739.
LWCL-FH8: Frances Wood was born in 1645 in Sudbury, in the Massachusetts Bay Colony, and died in 1718 in nearby Marlborough, Middlesex County.
LRFR-SR1: Rebecca Larkin was born in 1667 in Anne Arundel, Maryland, and died in 1718 in Baltimore, Maryland.
L45Q-WQ2: Colonel Samuel Prioleau was born in 1717 in Charleston, South Carolina. He was still living in Charleston in 1790, recorded in the St. Michael and St. Philip township, and died there in 1792.
L87P-XVJ: David Hext was born in 1686 in Charleston, South Carolina, where he also lived and died. He served in the military in the United States, and died in his home city in 1754.
KC1M-K8W: Johann Valentin Scholler was born in 1685 in Kusel, Bavaria, Germany, and died in 1768.
LV1R-3CK: Pierre Gibaut was born in 1620 in Lusignan, in the Vienne region of France, where he worked as a butcher. He married in France, and died in 1667 in Aisne.
G8LT-Z15: Madeleine Gaumond was born in Paris, France, in 1646. She crossed the Atlantic and arrived at Quebec City on 3 July 1668, joining the growing French settlement in Canada. She died in 1703.
